description_long: This is a traditional trident-style pitchfork with three metal tines, the central tine being straight and longer than the two outer tines, which curve outward. The tines are robust and appear to be forged from dark, possibly blackened steel, giving the tool a sturdy and utilitarian appearance. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped with a textured grip, likely rubber or a synthetic material, and features metallic bands for reinforcement near the base of the tines. The overall design is compact and balanced, suitable for one-handed use, and the proportions suggest it is intended for close-quarters work or as a defensive tool. The finish is smooth and slightly glossy, with no visible rust or wear, indicating it is well-maintained or new. The color palette is predominantly black with subtle metallic highlights and a touch of yellow or gold on the handle bands.
description: A compact, black trident-style pitchfork with three metal tines and a textured, reinforced handle featuring metallic bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three tines, with the central tine straight and the two outer tines curving outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the central tine and the slim profile of the handle and tines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle's end and the symmetrical curve of the outer tines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the long central tine and the handle's grip details.
